title: Walks

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_4 -- "Walks".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_4
% title: Walks
\def\rowref{def\_3\_4}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_4}

\begin{Def}[Walks]\label{def:walks}
     Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $v,w \in G$.
\begin{enumerate}
    \item A \emph{walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is a finite alternating sequence of adjacent nodes and edges 
      \[v=v_0, a_0,  v_1, \dots v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n=w\] 
%      \[v=v_0 \sus v_1 \sus  \cdots \sus v_{n-1} \sus v_n=w\]
        in $G$ for some $n \ge 0$, i.e.\ such that for every $k=0,\dots,n-1$ 
        we have that $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E \cup L$ or $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$, and with end nodes $v_0=v$ and $v_n=w$.
        An example walk from $v_0$ to $v_3$ could look like:
        \[v_0 \tuh v_1 \hut  v_2 \huh v_3, \qquad\text{ with } \qquad  v_0 \tuh v_1, v_2 \tuh v_1 \in E,\,v_2 \huh v_3 \in L. \]
        The same node may appear multiple times in a walk.
        Also the \emph{trivial walk} consisting of a single node $v_0 \in G$ is allowed (if $v=w$).
        The walk is called \emph{into $v_0$} if $a_0 = v_0 \hus v_1$, and \emph{out of $v_0$} if $a_0 = v_0 \tuh v_1$.
        Similarly, it is called \emph{into $v_n$} if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \suh v_n$ and \emph{out of $v_n$} if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \hut v_n$.
    \item A \emph{directed walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \tuh v_1 \tuh  \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$,
        where all arrowheads point in the direction of $w$ and there are no arrowheads pointing back.
   % \item[] Directed walks exclude the trivial walk per definition.
    \item A \emph{bidirected walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \huh v_1 \huh  \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \huh v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$, where all edges are bidirected.
    \item A \emph{collider walk} from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \suh v_1 \huh  \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \hus v_n=w,\]
        for some $n \ge 0$, where all nodes in between $v$ and $w$ have two arrowheads pointing towards them (a.k.a.\ collider).
        Note that for $n=1$ this reads: $v\sus w \in G$.
    \item A walk is called \emph{path} if no node occurs more than once.
    \item A \emph{bifurcation} between $v$ and $w$ in $G$ is a walk of the form:
        \[v=v_0 \hut v_1 \hut  \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \hus v_k  \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
   %             \[v=v_0 \hut v_1 \hut  \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \huh v_k  \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]
        such that $v \ne w$, the walk contains both endnodes exactly once, 
	the subwalk $v_0 \hut \cdots v_{k-1}$ is a directed walk from $v_{k-1}$ to $v_0$,
	and the subwalk $v_k \tuh \cdots v_n$ is a directed walk from $v_k$ to $v_n$.
        %every node has at most one arrowhead pointing towards it, and 
        %both endnodes have exactly one arrowhead pointing towards them. 
        If the edge $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ is directed ($v_{k-1} \hut v_k$) then we say that the bifurcation has \emph{source} $v_{k}$.
  \end{enumerate}
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $v,w \in G$.

  1. A walk from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is a finite alternating sequence of adjacent nodes and edges \[v=v_0, a_0, v_1, \dots v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n=w\]

    in $G$ for some $n \ge 0$, i.e.\ such that for every $k=0,\dots,n-1$ we have that $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E \cup L$ or $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$, and with end nodes $v_0=v$ and $v_n=w$. An example walk from $v_0$ to $v_3$ could look like: \[v_0 \tuh v_1 \hut v_2 \huh v_3, \qquad\text{ with } \qquad v_0 \tuh v_1, v_2 \tuh v_1 \in E,\,v_2 \huh v_3 \in L. \] The same node may appear multiple times in a walk. Also the trivial walk consisting of a single node $v_0 \in G$ is allowed (if $v=w$). The walk is called into $v_0$ if $a_0 = v_0 \hus v_1$, and out of $v_0$ if $a_0 = v_0 \tuh v_1$. Similarly, it is called into $v_n$ if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \suh v_n$ and out of $v_n$ if $a_{n-1} = v_{n-1} \hut v_n$.

  2. A directed walk from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form: \[v=v_0 \tuh v_1 \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\] for some $n \ge 0$, where all arrowheads point in the direction of $w$ and there are no arrowheads pointing back.
  3. A bidirected walk from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form: \[v=v_0 \huh v_1 \huh \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \huh v_n=w,\] for some $n \ge 0$, where all edges are bidirected.
  4. A collider walk from $v$ to $w$ in $G$ is of the form: \[v=v_0 \suh v_1 \huh \cdots \huh v_{n-1} \hus v_n=w,\] for some $n \ge 0$, where all nodes in between $v$ and $w$ have two arrowheads pointing towards them (a.k.a.\ collider). Note that for $n=1$ this reads: $v\sus w \in G$.
  5. A walk is called path if no node occurs more than once.
  6. A bifurcation between $v$ and $w$ in $G$ is a walk of the form: \[v=v_0 \hut v_1 \hut \cdots \hut v_{k-1} \hus v_k \tuh \cdots \tuh v_{n-1} \tuh v_n=w,\]

    such that $v \ne w$, the walk contains both endnodes exactly once, the subwalk $v_0 \hut \cdots v_{k-1}$ is a directed walk from $v_{k-1}$ to $v_0$, and the subwalk $v_k \tuh \cdots v_n$ is a directed walk from $v_k$ to $v_n$.

    If the edge $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ is directed ($v_{k-1} \hut v_k$) then we say that the bifurcation has source $v_{k}$.

lean_code_with_comments:
inductive EdgeStep (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | forward  {u v : Node} (h : G.tuh u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | backward {u v : Node} (h : G.hut u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | bidir    {u v : Node} (h : G.huh u v) : EdgeStep G u v

def Walk.support {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → List Node
  | .nil _ => [u]
  | .cons _ p => u :: p.support

def Walk.length {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → ℕ
  | .nil _ => 0
  | .cons _ p => p.length + 1

def Walk.lastHeadAtRight {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, _, .cons e' p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e' p

def Walk.lastTailAtRight {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, _, .cons e' p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e' p

def Walk.IsColliderTail {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .cons e' p => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .cons _ _ => False
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .cons _ _ => False

inductive HingeKind (G : CDMG Node) (u v : Node) : Type where
  | directed (h : G.hut u v) : HingeKind G u v
  | bidir    (h : G.huh u v) : HingeKind G u v

def HingeKind.isBidir {G : CDMG Node} {u v : Node} : HingeKind G u v → Prop
  | .directed _ => False
  | .bidir _    => True

inductive Walk (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | nil  {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G) : Walk G v v
  | cons {u v w : Node} (e : EdgeStep G u v) (p : Walk G v w) : Walk G u w

def Walk.intoV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => True

def Walk.outOfV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.intoVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e p

def Walk.outOfVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e p

def Walk.IsPath {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} (p : Walk G u w) : Prop :=
  p.support.Nodup

def Walk.IsDirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirectedWalk
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False

def Walk.IsBidirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) p => p.IsBidirectedWalk
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False

def Walk.IsColliderWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.nil _) => True
  | .cons (.forward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.backward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.forward _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.backward _) (.cons _ _) => False

inductive EdgeStep (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | forward  {u v : Node} (h : G.tuh u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | backward {u v : Node} (h : G.hut u v) : EdgeStep G u v
  | bidir    {u v : Node} (h : G.huh u v) : EdgeStep G u v
def Walk.support {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → List Node
  | .nil _ => [u]
  | .cons _ p => u :: p.support
def Walk.length {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → ℕ
  | .nil _ => 0
  | .cons _ p => p.length + 1
def Walk.lastHeadAtRight {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, _, .cons e' p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e' p
def Walk.lastTailAtRight {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, _, .cons e' p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e' p
def Walk.IsColliderTail {G : CDMG Node}
    : ∀ {u v w : Node}, EdgeStep G u v → Walk G v w → Prop
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .backward _, .nil _ => True
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .nil _ => False
  | _, _, _, .bidir _, .cons e' p => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | _, _, _, .forward _, .cons _ _ => False

inductive HingeKind (G : CDMG Node) (u v : Node) : Type where
  | directed (h : G.hut u v) : HingeKind G u v
  | bidir    (h : G.huh u v) : HingeKind G u v
def HingeKind.isBidir {G : CDMG Node} {u v : Node} : HingeKind G u v → Prop
  | .directed _ => False
  | .bidir _    => True
inductive Walk (G : CDMG Node) : Node → Node → Type _ where
  | nil  {v : Node} (hv : v ∈ G) : Walk G v v
  | cons {u v w : Node} (e : EdgeStep G u v) (p : Walk G v w) : Walk G u w
def Walk.intoV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => True
def Walk.outOfV0 {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => True
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False
def Walk.intoVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastHeadAtRight e p
def Walk.outOfVn {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => False
  | .cons e p => Walk.lastTailAtRight e p
def Walk.IsPath {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} (p : Walk G u w) : Prop :=
  p.support.Nodup
def Walk.IsDirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.forward _) p => p.IsDirectedWalk
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.bidir _) _ => False
def Walk.IsBidirectedWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) p => p.IsBidirectedWalk
  | .cons (.forward _) _ => False
  | .cons (.backward _) _ => False
def Walk.IsColliderWalk {G : CDMG Node} {u w : Node} : Walk G u w → Prop
  | .nil _ => True
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.nil _) => True
  | .cons (.forward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.backward _) (.nil _) => False
  | .cons (.forward _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.bidir _) (.cons e' p) => Walk.IsColliderTail e' p
  | .cons (.backward _) (.cons _ _) => False
addition_to_the_LN:
[bifurcation_right_chain_trivial_is_just_directed_walk] As an additional constraint on the bifurcation form, both endnodes $v_0$ and $v_n$ must have exactly one arrowhead pointing towards them. In particular, this excludes the degenerate case $k = n$ with directed hinge $v_{n-1} \hut v_n$, in which the walk reduces to a directed walk $v_0 \leftarrow v_1 \leftarrow \cdots \leftarrow v_n$ and $v_n$ has no arrowhead pointing towards it.

[collider_walk_n1_form_contradicts_inline_note] For $n=1$, a collider walk from $v$ to $w$ requires a bidirected edge $v \huh w \in E$ (arrowheads at both endpoints), as dictated by the form $v_0 \suh v_1$ combined with $v_{n-1} \hus v_n$ collapsing onto the single edge. The inline note "$v \sus w \in G$" is to be read in this stricter sense and does *not* admit purely directed edges $v \tuh w$ or $v \hut w$ as collider walks of length 1.
design_choices:
## Encoding the LN's `a_k` as a first-class `EdgeStep` ADT

**A three-constructor inductive `EdgeStep` reifies the LN edge symbol as data, not a `Prop`-side disjunction.** The lecture notes' `a_k` is a syntactic object whose shape (`tuh` / `hut` / `huh`) drives the into/out classifications and every specialised-walk predicate. A `Prop`-valued `Or` would erase which disjunct fired, forcing every downstream pattern match to peer through `Or.elim`; a single `G.sus` "any edge" step would collapse the trichotomy at the type level. Living in `Type _` also preserves the choice of constructor against proof irrelevance, so two walks with the same vertex sequence but different edge kinds remain distinct walks.

**Walk-direction names: `forward` / `backward` / `bidir`.** `forward` walks with the underlying directed edge, `backward` walks against it (the underlying edge is `(v,u) ∈ E`), `bidir` is the symmetric case. The constructors hold only the existence proof `h : G.tuh u v` etc.; redundant endpoint-membership fields are omitted because `CDMG.hE_subset` / `hL_subset` already force `u, v ∈ G`.

## `Walk` as a Mathlib-style indexed inductive

**`Walk G u w` mirrors `SimpleGraph.Walk` with `nil` carrying an explicit `hv : v ∈ G` and `cons` chaining an `EdgeStep` from the left.** A parallel-list structure (`vs`, `steps` with length invariants) and a `Fin n → Node` function encoding were both rejected because they make pattern matching on the first/last step painful and force index arithmetic for concatenation. Only `nil` carries a membership proof — `cons`'s `EdgeStep` already pins both endpoints into `G`. The `cons`-from-the-left convention propagates to `support`, `length`, and the `intoV0` / `outOfV0` versus `intoVn` / `outOfVn` split.

## Specialised walks as predicates, not separate inductives

**`IsDirectedWalk`, `IsBidirectedWalk`, `IsColliderWalk`, and `IsPath` are all `Prop`-valued predicates on `Walk`.** This means `def_3_5`'s `Anc^G` etc. map verbatim to `∃ p : Walk G w v, p.IsDirectedWalk` with no coercion, and `support` / `length` (and future `concat` / `reverse` / sub-walk) are written once on `Walk` and reused. Fresh inductives would require per-type recursors and coercions everywhere.

## The trivial walk is admitted everywhere

**`Walk.nil` vacuously satisfies all three specialised predicates and is a path.** The LN's literal "for some $n \ge 0$" admits $n = 0$; the LN source even contains a *commented-out* exclusion line above the directed-walk item, showing the author considered and rejected it. Downstream consumers that need a non-trivial witness (e.g. `def_3_6` acyclicity) add `0 < p.length` at the use site. The four into/out classifications uniformly return `False` on `nil`, formalising "no $a_0$ exists $\Rightarrow$ no classification applies".

## Auxiliary helpers for `Vn` and collider-tail recursion

**`lastHeadAtRight`, `lastTailAtRight`, and `IsColliderTail` take an explicit current `EdgeStep` plus a remaining tail walk, instead of using as-patterns on `Walk`.** A direct `p@(.cons _ _) => p.intoVn` shape elaborates but pushes the compiler into well-founded recursion, blocking `rfl`-reduction on base cases like `(Walk.nil _).intoVn = False`. Lifting the current step into an explicit parameter restores definitional reduction in every base case — a load-bearing property because chapter 5+ separation proofs rely on these predicates unfolding by `rfl`.

## Encoding the n=1 collider-walk addition strictly

**`Walk.IsColliderWalk` accepts a length-1 walk only when its single step is `.bidir`, rejecting both `.forward` and `.backward`.** The LN's formal pattern $v_0 \suh v_1 \hus v_n$ collapses for $n=1$ onto a single edge that must carry arrowheads at both endpoints. The LN's inline note "$v \sus w \in G$" superficially admits any edge, but the operator addition `[collider_walk_n1_form_contradicts_inline_note]` resolves the contradiction in favour of the form. This is the contractual locus where the addition is honoured on the type.

## Naming conventions and namespace placement

**`Walk`, `EdgeStep`, and their predicates live directly in the `Causality` namespace, not under `Causality.CDMG`.** They are vertex-level concepts about walks *between* nodes rather than field-style accessors on a graph; call sites read `p.IsDirectedWalk` and `p.support`, not `G.IsDirectedWalk p`. The `V0` / `Vn` suffix on the into/out predicates names the LN's $v_0$ / $v_n$ explicitly to leave room for later "into-an-arbitrary-vertex" notions.
